summary: Backend regression after server.py -> core/+routes/ split. Ran 26 new regression tests (test_refactor_regression.py, all 26 PASS) covering auth/refresh, /api/ready DB proxy, RBAC, leads CSV, enrichment pricing + business, payments checkout, threat (reports+outreach-profile roundtrip+scan), outreach (templates/auto/preview/sample-pack JSON+CSV+PDF), storefront (leads/sectors/rfp), public landing (waitlist/enrich-score/chat), governance/admin (tenants/audit/monitoring), intel/sources. Also re-ran existing backend_test.py (26/36 pass, 3 fail, 7 error). Refactor is largely behavior-preserving BUT exposed one real regression: POST /api/enrichment/enrich returns 500 because two EnrichReq Pydantic models are defined in server.py (lines 137 and 1582) with different fields; the second overrides the first, and routes/enrichment.py imports the overridden (wrong) one. Other backend_test.py failures are pre-existing/behavior changes unrelated to this refactor (register now assigns 'tenant_admin' instead of 'user' — matches PRD expectation of new tenant creation; scraper providers config changed to apify_reddit/overpass; apify quota exhausted).

critical.issue: 500 AttributeError: 'EnrichReq' object has no attribute 'batch'. Root cause: server.py has TWO class EnrichReq(BaseModel) definitions — line 137 (correct: lead_id/batch/limit) and line 1582 (wrong: category/only_hq/fl_only/limit for email enrichment). The second definition overrides the first at import; routes/enrichment.py `from server import EnrichReq` gets the wrong one, so body.batch/lead_id no longer exist.

minor.issue: Now returns role='tenant_admin' instead of 'user' (multi-tenant model). This is intentional per PRD ('creates its own tenant') but old backend_test.py fixture asserts role=='user'. Update existing tests, or expose an option to create a plain member for RBAC tests.
Apify plan out of usage ('not-enough-usage-to-run-paid-actor'), so scraper cycle can't complete in tests. Not a code regression.

action_items: Remove or rename the duplicate class EnrichReq at /app/backend/server.py:1582 (the email-enrichment one used by /email-enrichment/start). Rename to EmailEnrichReq (or similar) and update its usage site so routes/enrichment.py imports the intended lead-enrichment EnrichReq (line 137 with lead_id/batch/limit).
Update /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py user_account fixture to accept role in {'user','tenant_admin'} since register now creates a tenant (or add a /api/admin/users create-plain-member helper).
Optional: refresh scraper config test to accept the new provider set (apify_reddit/overpass) or add a compatibility layer.
critical_code_review_comments: Duplicate Pydantic model name (EnrichReq) in server.py is a footgun — the AST-based route split silently imports whichever definition wins. Add a lint check or split domain models into modules to prevent recurrence.
server.py is still 2342 lines — consider moving remaining models (scraper engine + models) into core/models.py so routes can import from a canonical location instead of the shared server namespace.
core/db.py db proxy works cleanly (all 26 regression tests pass, /api/ready returns db='connected'). No wiring regression detected.

success_rate.backend: 96% (26/26 new regression + 26/36 legacy; single real regression bug)

test_credentials: admin@nexus.io / nexus123 (works). buyer@test.io does not exist — used fresh registered users for RBAC tests.
seed_data_creation: Fresh users registered per session via /api/auth/register with uuid emails; TEST_ prefixed leads created and deleted in existing tests. RFP with agency_name='TEST_Regression Agency' inserted (leftover, low volume).
context_for_next_testing_agent: New regression suite lives at /app/backend/tests/test_refactor_regression.py — 26 tests, all green. Legacy backend_test.py has fixture assertion baked in for role=='user' that no longer matches (post-tenancy: registration returns tenant_admin). Do not confuse this with the refactor. The single real regression is duplicate EnrichReq class in server.py breaking POST /api/enrichment/enrich (500). Outreach POST endpoints (send/auto/run/auto-sweep) intentionally NOT tested to avoid live Resend email sends.
rca of the issue: During the AST-driven monolith->modules split, server.py was left with two `class EnrichReq(BaseModel)` definitions (line 137 lead-enrichment shape vs line 1582 email-enrichment shape). Python's last-definition-wins semantics means `from server import EnrichReq` in routes/enrichment.py resolves to the second (email) model, which lacks lead_id/batch. When /api/enrichment/enrich is called it raises AttributeError on body.batch -> 500. Fix: rename the email-enrichment model to EmailEnrichReq (line 1582) and update its consumer (the email-enrichment endpoint). Reproduction: `curl -X POST $BASE/api/enrichment/enrich -H 'Cookie: access_token=…' -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"lead_id":"<any>"}'` -> 500.
